content.content:
limits:
      cpu: 200m
      memory: 256Mi
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
    port: 80
    targetPort: 8080

# Prometheus ServiceMonitor (Phase 4 D-O6).
#
# DEFAULT OFF per CLAUDE.md anti-pattern:
#   "Default the chart's ServiceMonitor to prometheus.enabled=false to avoid
#    CRD-not-found on plain clusters."
#
# Enable explicitly via `--set prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` once
# the prometheus-operator CRDs (monitoring.coreos.com/v1) are installed.
prometheus:
  # PromQL-proxy endpoint (Phase 4 telemetry surface -- milestone-01 Q1 lock-in).
  #
  # Q1 DECISION: PROXY, not a second browser-side datasource. This is a
  # SERVER-SIDE value. When non-empty it is injected as the PROM_ENDPOINT env
  # var into the dashboard container (templates/dashboard-deployment.yaml, landed
  # by plan-02), and the dashboard chi server proxies PromQL via
  # GET /api/v1/query and GET /api/v1/query_range. The browser therefore never
  # learns a second network target: single origin, no CORS, one auth surface,
  # and the Prometheus URL stays confined to the dashboard process (matters when
  # Prometheus is ClusterIP-only and unreachable from outside the cluster).
  #
  # Empty (default) => graceful degradation: the proxy returns HTTP 200 with the
  # body {"status":"unavailable"} and the React Telemetry tab falls back to
  # live-only CRD-.status views (no error state, no blank panel, no broken
  # layout). Set e.g.
  #   --set prometheus.endpoint=http://prometheus-operated.monitoring:9090
  # to wire historical cost-over-time charts.
  endpoint: ""
  # Prometheus local-TSDB retention window for telemetry history (Phase 4 -- Q3).
  #
  # This chart ships a ServiceMonitor only (serviceMonitor block below) and does
  # NOT bundle a Prometheus server, so this value configures no in-chart
  # component. It is the single documented knob for the retention operators must
  # apply as --storage.tsdb.retention.time=<value> on their operator-managed
  # Prometheus.
  #
  # RETENTION MATH: one multi-day dogfood run plus a one-week post-run analysis
  # window fits comfortably inside the 15d default. For organizations tracking
  # cost-over-time trends ACROSS multiple runs (weeks of continuous dogfooding),
  # set "30d" or longer so the cost-over-time charts span the full cross-run
  # history.
  retentionTime: "15d"
  serviceMonitor:
    enabled: false
    interval: 30s
    scrapeTimeout: 10s

# OpenTelemetry env-driven config (Phase 4 D-O3 / plan 04-03).
#
# Read by internal/otelinit at controller boot:
#   - exporter.endpoint = ""  → no-op TracerProvider (zero overhead; default)
#   - exporter.endpoint = "otel-collector:4317" → OTLP gRPC exporter
#
# Sampler is env-driven (NOT WithSampler in code, per Pitfall 24 mitigation):
# OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER + OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG. Defaults trace 10% of root
# spans (`parentbased_traceidratio` arg `0.1`).
otel:
  exporter:
    endpoint: ""
  tracesSampler: "parentbased_traceidratio"
  tracesSamplerArg: "0.1"
  serviceName: "tide-controller-manager"

# Single shared RWX PVC for all Projects' workspaces (Blocker #2/#3 fix —
# RESEARCH.md Open Question #2 RESOLVED with the single-shared-PVC + subPath architecture).
#
# Architecture: ONE chart-provisioned tide-projects PVC (ReadWriteMany), Helm-templated at
# install time via charts/tide/templates/projects-pvc.yaml.
#   - Manager Pod mounts PVC at /workspaces (no subPath) — reads /workspaces/{project-uid}/workspace/...
#   - Task Pods mount PVC with subPath: {project-uid}/workspace → appearing as /workspace
#   - Init Job (Plan 10) mounts PVC with subPath: {project-uid}/workspace and mkdirs the layout

# quotas are Project.Spec.Budget-driven (FAIL-04) not storage-driven.
# Phase 3 ART-02 evaluates per-Project PVCs if/when this becomes a real concern.
#
# storageClassName: "" uses the cluster-default StorageClass (suitable for kind/minikube/dev).

    storageClassName: ""  # cluster default; set to a CSI RWX driver for production

#     - tide-customer-globex
projectNamespaces: []

content.content:
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Working Rules
6	
7	Project-agnostic operating principles. Apply these before anything else, including before reading the rest of this file.
8	
9	### 1. Observe First
10	
11	Every wasted iteration in Phase 02.2 traced back to acting on assumptions instead of observations. The cascade-5 debug session hypothesized a "Wave Ready check" that didn't exist; the cascade-8 debug session hypothesized "ResourceVersion-conflict exponential-backoff suppression" when the actual cause was a never-assigned `Dispatcher` field. Both were avoidable.
12	
13	1. When a runtime gate is BLOCKED → **read the manager log (`kubectl logs` or `/tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log`) and the VERIFICATION.md frontmatter BEFORE hypothesizing**
14	2. When a controller misbehaves → **`kubectl logs` + `kubectl describe <crd>` + `kubectl get <crd> -o yaml` BEFORE reading reconciler source**
15	3. When a debug hypothesis cites a specific code path → **grep the codebase to confirm it exists in the cited form** (`grep -nE "Wave.*Ready|Ready.*Wave" internal/controller/` would have refuted cascade-5's framing in seconds)
16	4. When a code change has no visible effect → **verify the binary rebuilt (image digest changed) and the Pod rotated (`kubectl get pods -n tide-system` shows new age)**, don't keep tweaking
17	5. When suggesting a Makefile target → **`grep -nE '^target-name:' Makefile`** to confirm it exists before recommending the command
18	6. When an agent could answer a question → **but `kubectl get`, `git log --grep`, or a single grep answers it faster, use the CLI**
19	
20	Subagents are for understanding architecture and authoring artifacts. Runtime artifacts (manager logs, CRD status, git history) are for debugging runtime failures. Do not confuse the two.
21	
22	### 2. Execute, Don't Ask
23	
24	When the next step is obvious and non-destructive AND inside an active GSD workflow: do it. Don't present the command and ask for confirmation. This includes running `make test-int`, rerunning failing tests, creating planning artifacts, applying fixes specified in an approved `PLAN.md`, and committing per the executor's task protocol.
25	
26	**Exceptions — always confirm or refuse:**
27	
28	- **Production code edits outside an active GSD plan.** The `GSD Workflow Enforcement` section below is binding. Even if the fix is obvious, route through `/gsd:quick` or `/gsd:debug` so artifacts stay in sync.
29	- **Edits to `charts/tide/values.yaml`** — chart is FIXED contract per Phase 02.2's anti-pattern. Binary catches up to chart, never reverse.
30	- **`git push --force`** to any branch, especially `main`.
31	- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.
32	- **`kind delete cluster`** outside an explicit clean-rerun sequence (it destroys all test state).
33	- **`rm -rf`** on `.git/`, `.planning/`, `.claude/`, or any directory containing uncommitted user work.
34	- **`gsd-sdk query state-set ...`** that downgrades phase status or rewrites tracked progress fields.
35	
36	### 3. Verify Before Claiming
37	
38	Never say "this should fix it," "the fix is complete," or "cascade-N is closed." Run the verification. Read the gate_decision. Grep for the artifact. Then state what you observed.
39	
40	Concrete verification protocols for Phase 02.2-style runtime gates:
41	
42	- **Plan landed:** `git log --oneline -1 -- <files_modified>` shows the expected commit on `main`
43	- **VERIFICATION.md exists:** `ls .planning/phases/<dir>/<plan>-VERIFICATION.md` returns a file
44	- **Gate APPROVED:** `grep -cE '^gate_decision: APPROVED$' <VERIFICATION>` returns exactly `1`
45	- **Test ran:** `tail -30 /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` shows Ginkgo summary line `Ran X Specs in Ys`
46	- **Dispatch is live (Phase 02.2-10 evidence shape):** `grep -E '"creating job"|"dispatch"' /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` returns ≥ 1 line, AND `kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces` shows Jobs in the test namespaces
47	- **Cascade closed:** the specific manager-log error that defined the cascade (e.g. `"no project found in namespace X"`) returns 0 in the new run's log
48	- **`make test-int` exit ≠ "Ginkgo green."** The `test/integration/kind` package bundles plain go-tests (e.g. the helm-template contract tests like `TestHelmDeploymentTemplateRendersManagerPodAnnotations`) alongside the Ginkgo specs. One RED go-test fails the package and trips `make test-int` non-zero **even when both layers print `Ran X of Y … SUCCESS!`**. Always read the echoed `MAKE_EXIT` AND `grep -nE '^--- FAIL|^FAIL\s'` the log — not just the Ginkgo summary. (Phase 7 nearly tagged v1.0 on "14/14 green" while `make test-int` was red on a dropped chart-template block.)
49	- **A subagent's "pre-existing / unrelated" dismissal of a failing test is a claim, not verification.** Before relaying it as safe, git-archaeology the regressing commit (`git log -S '<token>' -- <file>`) and confirm it does not fail the gate. (Two Phase-7 agents waved off the dropped `podAnnotations` render block as pre-existing; it was a real ship-blocker — the verifier caught it and a `make test-int` re-run proved it.)
50	
51	## Operating Notes (Phase 02.2 lessons)
52	
53	Rules learned from 10 BLOCKED iterations across Phase 02.2. Trust them.
54	
55	- **Don't predict "chain terminator."** Plans 02.2-08/09/10 each predicted the end; all surfaced new cascades. Frame work as iterations; don't anchor on terminations.
56	- **Pre-empt worktree cwd-drift before merging.** Executor agents occasionally write artifacts to main's working tree instead of the worktree's. Pre-merge check:
57	  ```
58	  UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | grep '^??' | grep "<plan>-" || true)
59	  ```
60	  If non-empty, diff against the worktree branch's commits; remove duplicates before `git merge`. (Plan 02.2-09 merge failed without this; Plan 02.2-10 succeeded with it.)
61	- **`gsd-sdk query state.begin-phase` resets STATE.md body fields.** It re-derives `Current Position` and `Status` from current plan inventory. Edit body fields AFTER `state.begin-phase` runs, not before, or the edit gets clobbered.
62	- **Question implicit framings when cascade classes diverge.** If 3+ consecutive cascades are the same class (e.g. cascades 2/3 both flag-mismatches), trust tactical iteration. If consecutive cascades differ in class (e.g. cascade-4 budget → cascade-5 harness-bug → cascade-6 timeouts), surface "is the underlying assumption still right?" before authoring the next plan. The cascade-8 debug session forced this question 7 plans late.
63	- **Use `--wave N` filter when a closeout plan is gated on a BLOCKED artifact.** Plan 02.2-02's `checkpoint:human-verify` reads `gate_decision: APPROVED` from a verification artifact; `--auto` mode would false-approve against a BLOCKED gate. Filter dispatch with `--wave <new-gap-wave>` until any plan records APPROVED and the rewire fires.
64	- **Subagent prompts: focus, don't restate.** Agents have their own system prompts that cover anti-patterns and protocols. `<additional_context>` blocks should carry decision-only context (the specific gap, the user's choice, the corrected hypothesis) — not the full cascade history or the entire anti-pattern table. Token budget directly affects subagent quality.
65	- **Honor `workflow.auto_advance: true` literally** — fire the chain across phase boundaries. But ASK FIRST at fix-shape decision points (Option A vs B at scope-defining moments). The user memory note covers this; respect it.
66	- **Decorative banners are noise unless they signal a real workflow transition.** `━━━ GSD ► STAGE ━━━` belongs at PLANNING / VERIFYING / EXECUTING boundaries (per `references/ui-brand.md`), not on every status update. Routine progress can be one-line.
67	- **End-of-turn summary: one or two sentences.** What changed and what's next. Resist the urge to recap multi-section status when the conversation thread is fresh.
68	- **Use TaskCreate for multi-cycle workflows.** A plan-check-commit-execute loop repeated 5+ times in one session genuinely benefits from task tracking. The system reminders about TaskCreate are not boilerplate.
69	- **STATE.md "Current Position" body text drifts.** It's prose, not derived; updates to it need to happen explicitly. The frontmatter (`progress.completed_plans`) is computed by SDK verbs; the body text is not.
70	- **Constrained-VM full-suite recipe (the dev Docker VM is ~7.65 GiB).** It DOES fit a clean `make test-int` if you don't accumulate state: delete → recreate → pre-warm (provisioner Ready + `kind load busybox:1.36`) a fresh kind cluster per heavy run, one heavy run at a time. Never let `make acceptance-v1-smoke` (it spins its OWN `tide-acceptance-<ts>` cluster) run while a `tide-test` cluster is still up — two single-node clusters OOM the node (exit 137). Phase 7's "env-gated, needs a bigger VM" deferral was actually solvable this way on the same 7.65 GiB host.
71	- **The acceptance `$0` path is a different deploy surface than Layer B — peel it one layer at a time.** Layer B fixtures carry a dummy `providerSecretRef` and use the test harness's helm install; the `$0` small-project has none and uses the chart defaults. So `$0` acceptance surfaces bugs Layer B masks (cascade-12 chart `default "latest"` image tags; cascade-13 credproxy hard-requiring `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`). Expect each green layer to expose the next; don't predict the terminator.
72	
73	## What this repository is
74	
75	**TIDE** (Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution) — a Kubernetes-native orchestrator for hierarchical agentic coding work, designed to be open-sourced and to run against any project/codebase in any cluster. `README.md` is the design spec (and doubles as the public-facing README); check `git log` and `.planning/` for current implementation state.
76	
77	## The spec is load-bearing
78	
79	Everything downstream — schemas, APIs, controller logic, persistence — should trace back to the paradigm doc. When designing or implementing, preserve these distinctions; they exist for reasons the doc argues for explicitly:

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Working Rules

Project-agnostic operating principles. Apply these before anything else, including before reading the rest of this file.

### 1. Observe First

Every wasted iteration in Phase 02.2 traced back to acting on assumptions instead of observations. The cascade-5 debug session hypothesized a "Wave Ready check" that didn't exist; the cascade-8 debug session hypothesized "ResourceVersion-conflict exponential-backoff suppression" when the actual cause was a never-assigned `Dispatcher` field. Both were avoidable.

1. When a runtime gate is BLOCKED → **read the manager log (`kubectl logs` or `/tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log`) and the VERIFICATION.md frontmatter BEFORE hypothesizing**
2. When a controller misbehaves → **`kubectl logs` + `kubectl describe <crd>` + `kubectl get <crd> -o yaml` BEFORE reading reconciler source**
3. When a debug hypothesis cites a specific code path → **grep the codebase to confirm it exists in the cited form** (`grep -nE "Wave.*Ready|Ready.*Wave" internal/controller/` would have refuted cascade-5's framing in seconds)
4. When a code change has no visible effect → **verify the binary rebuilt (image digest changed) and the Pod rotated (`kubectl get pods -n tide-system` shows new age)**, don't keep tweaking
5. When suggesting a Makefile target → **`grep -nE '^target-name:' Makefile`** to confirm it exists before recommending the command
6. When an agent could answer a question → **but `kubectl get`, `git log --grep`, or a single grep answers it faster, use the CLI**

Subagents are for understanding architecture and authoring artifacts. Runtime artifacts (manager logs, CRD status, git history) are for debugging runtime failures. Do not confuse the two.

### 2. Execute, Don't Ask

When the next step is obvious and non-destructive AND inside an active GSD workflow: do it. Don't present the command and ask for confirmation. This includes running `make test-int`, rerunning failing tests, creating planning artifacts, applying fixes specified in an approved `PLAN.md`, and committing per the executor's task protocol.

**Exceptions — always confirm or refuse:**

- **Production code edits outside an active GSD plan.** The `GSD Workflow Enforcement` section below is binding. Even if the fix is obvious, route through `/gsd:quick` or `/gsd:debug` so artifacts stay in sync.
- **Edits to `charts/tide/values.yaml`** — chart is FIXED contract per Phase 02.2's anti-pattern. Binary catches up to chart, never reverse.
- **`git push --force`** to any branch, especially `main`.
- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.
- **`kind delete cluster`** outside an explicit clean-rerun sequence (it destroys all test state).
- **`rm -rf`** on `.git/`, `.planning/`, `.claude/`, or any directory containing uncommitted user work.
- **`gsd-sdk query state-set ...`** that downgrades phase status or rewrites tracked progress fields.

### 3. Verify Before Claiming

Never say "this should fix it," "the fix is complete," or "cascade-N is closed." Run the verification. Read the gate_decision. Grep for the artifact. Then state what you observed.

Concrete verification protocols for Phase 02.2-style runtime gates:

- **Plan landed:** `git log --oneline -1 -- <files_modified>` shows the expected commit on `main`
- **VERIFICATION.md exists:** `ls .planning/phases/<dir>/<plan>-VERIFICATION.md` returns a file
- **Gate APPROVED:** `grep -cE '^gate_decision: APPROVED$' <VERIFICATION>` returns exactly `1`
- **Test ran:** `tail -30 /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` shows Ginkgo summary line `Ran X Specs in Ys`
- **Dispatch is live (Phase 02.2-10 evidence shape):** `grep -E '"creating job"|"dispatch"' /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` returns ≥ 1 line, AND `kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces` shows Jobs in the test namespaces
- **Cascade closed:** the specific manager-log error that defined the cascade (e.g. `"no project found in namespace X"`) returns 0 in the new run's log
- **`make test-int` exit ≠ "Ginkgo green."** The `test/integration/kind` package bundles plain go-tests (e.g. the helm-template contract tests like `TestHelmDeploymentTemplateRendersManagerPodAnnotations`) alongside the Ginkgo specs. One RED go-test fails the package and trips `make test-int` non-zero **even when both layers print `Ran X of Y … SUCCESS!`**. Always read the echoed `MAKE_EXIT` AND `grep -nE '^--- FAIL|^FAIL\s'` the log — not just the Ginkgo summary. (Phase 7 nearly tagged v1.0 on "14/14 green" while `make test-int` was red on a dropped chart-template block.)
- **A subagent's "pre-existing / unrelated" dismissal of a failing test is a claim, not verification.** Before relaying it as safe, git-archaeology the regressing commit (`git log -S '<token>' -- <file>`) and confirm it does not fail the gate. (Two Phase-7 agents waved off the dropped `podAnnotations` render block as pre-existing; it was a real ship-blocker — the verifier caught it and a `make test-int` re-run proved it.)

## Operating Notes (Phase 02.2 lessons)

Rules learned from 10 BLOCKED iterations across Phase 02.2. Trust them.

- **Don't predict "chain terminator."** Plans 02.2-08/09/10 each predicted the end; all surfaced new cascades. Frame work as iterations; don't anchor on terminations.
- **Pre-empt worktree cwd-drift before merging.** Executor agents occasionally write artifacts to main's working tree instead of the worktree's. Pre-merge check:
  ```
  UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | grep '^??' | grep "<plan>-" || true)
  ```
  If non-empty, diff against the worktree branch's commits; remove duplicates before `git merge`. (Plan 02.2-09 merge failed without this; Plan 02.2-10 succeeded with it.)
- **`gsd-sdk query state.begin-phase` resets STATE.md body fields.** It re-derives `Current Position` and `Status` from current plan inventory. Edit body fields AFTER `state.begin-phase` runs, not before, or the edit gets clobbered.
- **Question implicit framings when cascade classes diverge.** If 3+ consecutive cascades are the same class (e.g. cascades 2/3 both flag-mismatches), trust tactical iteration. If consecutive cascades differ in class (e.g. cascade-4 budget → cascade-5 harness-bug → cascade-6 timeouts), surface "is the underlying assumption still right?" before authoring the next plan. The cascade-8 debug session forced this question 7 plans late.
- **Use `--wave N` filter when a closeout plan is gated on a BLOCKED artifact.** Plan 02.2-02's `checkpoint:human-verify` reads `gate_decision: APPROVED` from a verification artifact; `--auto` mode would false-approve against a BLOCKED gate. Filter dispatch with `--wave <new-gap-wave>` until any plan records APPROVED and the rewire fires.
- **Subagent prompts: focus, don't restate.** Agents have their own system prompts that cover anti-patterns and protocols. `<additional_context>` blocks should carry decision-only context (the specific gap, the user's choice, the corrected hypothesis) — not the full cascade history or the entire anti-pattern table. Token budget directly affects subagent quality.
- **Honor `workflow.auto_advance: true` literally** — fire the chain across phase boundaries. But ASK FIRST at fix-shape decision points (Option A vs B at scope-defining moments). The user memory note covers this; respect it.
- **Decorative banners are noise unless they signal a real workflow transition.** `━━━ GSD ► STAGE ━━━` belongs at PLANNING / VERIFYING / EXECUTING boundaries (per `references/ui-brand.md`), not on every status update. Routine progress can be one-line.
- **End-of-turn summary: one or two sentences.** What changed and what's next. Resist the urge to recap multi-section status when the conversation thread is fresh.
- **Use TaskCreate for multi-cycle workflows.** A plan-check-commit-execute loop repeated 5+ times in one session genuinely benefits from task tracking. The system reminders about TaskCreate are not boilerplate.
- **STATE.md "Current Position" body text drifts.** It's prose, not derived; updates to it need to happen explicitly. The frontmatter (`progress.completed_plans`) is computed by SDK verbs; the body text is not.
- **Constrained-VM full-suite recipe (the dev Docker VM is ~7.65 GiB).** It DOES fit a clean `make test-int` if you don't accumulate state: delete → recreate → pre-warm (provisioner Ready + `kind load busybox:1.36`) a fresh kind cluster per heavy run, one heavy run at a time. Never let `make acceptance-v1-smoke` (it spins its OWN `tide-acceptance-<ts>` cluster) run while a `tide-test` cluster is still up — two single-node clusters OOM the node (exit 137). Phase 7's "env-gated, needs a bigger VM" deferral was actually solvable this way on the same 7.65 GiB host.
- **The acceptance `$0` path is a different deploy surface than Layer B — peel it one layer at a time.** Layer B fixtures carry a dummy `providerSecretRef` and use the test harness's helm install; the `$0` small-project has none and uses the chart defaults. So `$0` acceptance surfaces bugs Layer B masks (cascade-12 chart `default "latest"` image tags; cascade-13 credproxy hard-requiring `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`). Expect each green layer to expose the next; don't predict the terminator.

## What this repository is

**TIDE** (Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution) — a Kubernetes-native orchestrator for hierarchical agentic coding work, designed to be open-sourced and to run against any project/codebase in any cluster. `README.md` is the design spec (and doubles as the public-facing README); check `git log` and `.planning/` for current implementation state.

## The spec is load-bearing

Everything downstream — schemas, APIs, controller logic, persistence — should trace back to the paradigm doc. When designing or implementing, preserve these distinctions; they exist for reasons the doc argues for explicitly:

249	// Usage carries the token and cost tally reported by the harness in
250	// EnvelopeOut. Rolled up by the controller into Project.Status.budget at Task
251	// completion (D-D2).
252	type Usage struct {
253		// InputTokens is the total input tokens consumed across all LLM calls
254		// during this task's execution.
255		InputTokens int64 `json:"inputTokens"`
256	
257		// OutputTokens is the total output tokens produced across all LLM calls
258		// during this task's execution.
259		OutputTokens int64 `json:"outputTokens"`
260	
261		// EstimatedCostCents is the estimated US-cent cost for this task, rounded
262		// up to the nearest cent. Used for Project.Status.budget.costSpentCents
263		// rollup.
264		EstimatedCostCents int64 `json:"estimatedCostCents"`
265	
266		// Iterations is the actual number of agent loop iterations completed.
267		Iterations int `json:"iterations"`
268	
269		// CacheReadTokens is the count of input tokens served from the provider's
270		// prompt cache (D-C5). The Anthropic stream-json
271		// usage.cache_read_input_tokens field maps directly here. Surfacing this
272		// separately lets Project.Status.budget (Phase 2 D-D2) show the cached
273		// vs uncached input mix instead of hiding cache hits inside InputTokens.
274		CacheReadTokens int64 `json:"cacheReadTokens"`
275	
276		// CacheCreationTokens is the count of input tokens spent populating the
277		// provider's prompt cache during this dispatch (D-C5). The Anthropic
278		// stream-json usage.cache_creation_input_tokens field maps directly here.
279		// Cache creation is billed at a higher rate than cache reads; separating
280		// the two prevents budget rollups from understating real spend on
281		// cache-warmup turns.
282		CacheCreationTokens int64 `json:"cacheCreationTokens"`
283	}
284	
285	// DispatchMeta carries orchestrator-side dispatch metadata the planner subagent
286	// reads to build child-CRD references without querying the K8s API. It is kept
287	// separate from [ProviderSpec.Params] (model-parameters-only) so the anthropic
288	// runner's strict param allow-list is not tripped by dispatch metadata. Real
289	// Claude-backed subagent images may ignore this struct — the real planner
290	// authors child CRDs from the outcome prompt, not canned parent refs.
291	type DispatchMeta struct {
292		// ParentName is the up-stack CRD's metadata.name. The stub planner stamps
293		// it into the child CRD's parent-spec ref (projectRef / milestoneRef /
294		// phaseRef / planRef) so the controller can wire ownership without the
295		// subagent holding a K8s client (T-07-03-03: parentName is metadata, not a
296		// secret).
297		ParentName string `json:"parentName,omitempty"`
298	}

content.content:
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
# Build the claude-subagent runtime image.
# Invoked from REPO ROOT:
#   docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:test \
#                -f images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile .
#
# Stage 1: compile a static Linux Go binary.
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.26-alpine@sha256:a6a091eac01ceac4b97496fe2957a49b6cdd83365337d5f46f6f73710424e805 AS builder
ARG TARGETOS
ARG TARGETARCH

WORKDIR /workspace

# Cache the dependency layer before copying source.
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod go mod download

# Copy only what the binary needs (see .dockerignore for exclusions).
COPY pkg/dispatch/ pkg/dispatch/
COPY internal/harness/ internal/harness/
COPY internal/subagent/ internal/subagent/
COPY pkg/git/ pkg/git/
COPY cmd/claude-subagent/ cmd/claude-subagent/

# Static binary: no CGO, stripped symbols for a smaller image.
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=${TARGETOS:-linux} GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
      -ldflags="-s -w" \
      -o /out/claude-subagent \
      ./cmd/claude-subagent

# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
#
# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.
FROM node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732

RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    # The per-Project repo (/workspace/repo.git) is cloned by the tide-push Job
    # (uid 65532) but the executor runs as uid 1000 — git 2.35+ refuses a repo
    # it does not own ("detected dubious ownership"). The pod is the trust
    # boundary (hermetic, single-purpose), so mark all repos safe at the system
    # level. This covers our `git worktree add` shell-out AND any git the claude

# Build the claude-subagent runtime image.

ARG TARGETOS

COPY internal/subagent/ internal/subagent/
COPY pkg/git/ pkg/git/
COPY cmd/claude-subagent/ cmd/claude-subagent/

      -ldflags="-s -w" \
      -o /out/claude-subagent \
      ./cmd/claude-subagent

# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
#
# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.
FROM node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732

RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    # The per-Project repo (/workspace/repo.git) is cloned by the tide-push Job
    # (uid 65532) but the executor runs as uid 1000 — git 2.35+ refuses a repo

    # level. This covers our `git worktree add` shell-out AND any git the claude
    # CLI itself runs inside the worktree (whose .git points back to repo.git).
    && git config --system --add safe.directory '*'

RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.142

COPY --from=builder /out/claude-subagent /usr/local/bin/claude-subagent

# Run as non-root UID 1000 — matches D-G3 subagent harness UID requirement.

## Phase Brief — `phase-06-hetero-integration`

**Milestone

:** `milestone-03-hetero-integration`
**Phase:** `phase-06-hetero-integration`
**Role:** Milestone
join barrier — authors no new feature scope
; stitches phases 01–05
into a provably working heterogeneous dispatch story

.

---

### Objective

Demonstrate that a TIDE operator can configure
a single Project whose planner pool runs Claude and
whose executor pool runs Codex, apply it, and watch a
multi-phase run dispatch to completion with wave-boundary failure
semantics that are byte-for-byte identical regardless of which provider ran the failing task
. Codex spend surfaces on `tide_
cost_cents_total` and counts against the budget cap
— no silent $0 attribution. Every `
make verify-*` guard is green and

`make test` exits 0.

---

### Scope
